title: RECORD
description: Regulatory Evidence Capture of Reasoning and Decisions — an OpenInference extension for regulatory compliance in financial AI agents.

record_id.description: [REQUIRED] Globally unique identifier for this RECORD trace. Must be a UUIDv4.

timestamp.description: [REQUIRED] ISO 8601 UTC timestamp marking when the agent decision was finalised.

agent_id.description: [REQUIRED] Stable identifier for the agent system (e.g. service name or ARN).

agent_version.description: [REQUIRED] Semantic version of the agent model/code that produced this record.

authority_basis.description: [REQUIRED] One or more regulatory obligations that govern this decision. Use dot-notation: <framework>.<article_or_section>.

operating_domain.description: [REQUIRED] Degree of human oversight under which the agent operated for this decision.

influencing_parameters.description: [RECOMMENDED] Factors, features, or signals that materially influenced the agent's reasoning. Each entry should capture the factor name, its value at decision time, and a normalised weight (0–1).

factor.description: Human-readable name of the feature or signal.

value.description: Value of the factor at decision time. May be any JSON scalar.

weight.description: Normalised importance weight (0–1). Interpretation depends on weight_source.

weight_source.description: How the weight was derived. ml_model: upstream statistical model (SHAP, feature importance). llm_self_reported: the LLM agent explicitly named this factor as decisive in its chain_of_thought. rule_engine: a deterministic rule assigned a fixed priority. hybrid: combination of sources.

policy_evaluation.description: [RECOMMENDED] Ordered list of policy rules evaluated during the decision. Captures rule identity, outcome, and the agent's confidence.

rule_id.description: Unique identifier for the policy rule (e.g. 'RTS6-P1-ORDER-ENTRY').

confidence.description: Agent's confidence in the verdict (0 = uncertain, 1 = certain).

notes.description: Free-text explanation of why the verdict was reached.

decision_lineage.description: [RECOMMENDED] Pointers linking this RECORD to upstream traces, cases, or investigation threads for full audit-chain reconstruction.

parent_trace_id.description: OpenInference / OpenTelemetry trace ID of the parent span, if any.

case_id.description: Case or alert ID in the upstream case-management system.

investigation_thread_id.description: Identifier of a multi-step investigation thread grouping related RECORDs.

data_at_decision.description: [RECOMMENDED] Inventory of external data sources queried before the decision. Payload hashes enable bit-exact reconstruction; previews aid reviewer comprehension.

source.description: Logical name or URI of the data source.

query.description: Exact query or filter sent to the source.

payload_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the raw response payload.

payload_preview.description: Optional first 512 characters of the response for reviewer convenience.

human_disposition.description: [RECOMMENDED when operating_domain = supervised] Records the human analyst's final decision when a human reviewed the agent's recommendation.

action.description: Whether the human overrode, confirmed, or abstained from acting on the agent's recommendation.

rationale.description: Free-text justification provided by the human analyst.

analyst_id.description: Pseudonymous or hashed identifier of the analyst (do not store PII in plain text).

reviewed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the human review.

outcome_linkage.description: [RECOMMENDED] Links the decision to a downstream outcome or workflow artefact for closed-loop auditability.

reference_id.description: External identifier of the downstream artefact.

status.description: Current lifecycle status of the downstream artefact.

url.description: Optional deep-link to the artefact in the downstream system.

prompt.description: [REQUIRED] Complete input instruction delivered to the agent, including any system prompt, injected context, and user request. Verbatim capture is required for reproducibility.

chain_of_thought.description: [RECOMMENDED] Ordered sequence of intermediate reasoning steps produced by the agent before reaching its final action.

step.description: 1-based sequence number of this reasoning step.

content.description: Verbatim or summarised text of this reasoning step.

type.description: Optional semantic label for the reasoning step.

tools_accessed.description: [RECOMMENDED] Log of external tools or APIs called during the agent's reasoning, in call order.

tool_name.description: Logical name of the tool or API.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the tool invocation.

query.description: Arguments or query string passed to the tool.

tool_results.description: [RECOMMENDED] Responses returned by each tool call. Parallel array to tools_accessed; tool_name is used as the join key.

tool_name.description: Must match a tool_name entry in tools_accessed.

payload.description: Full or summarised response returned by the tool. May be any JSON value.

error.description: Error message if the tool call failed.

final_action.description: [REQUIRED] The agent's ultimate recommendation or decision for this case.

disposition.description: Free-text summary of what the agent decided and why.

severity_score.description: Optional 0–10 risk or severity score supporting the disposition.

escalated.description: Whether the agent escalated this case to a human or higher-tier system.

escalation_reason.description: If escalated=true, the machine-readable or free-text reason for escalation.

_comments.description: [OPTIONAL] Human-readable annotation block explaining how this RECORD document was produced. Keys correspond to field names; values describe what the field contains and which system or layer produced it. Ignored by validators and consumers — present only for developer comprehension.

openinference_span_id.description: [OPTIONAL] OpenInference / OpenTelemetry span ID for correlation with the parent observability platform.

model_id.description: [OPTIONAL] Identifier of the foundation model used (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-6', 'gpt-4o-2024-05-13').

jurisdiction.description: [OPTIONAL] ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or IANA jurisdiction tag indicating primary regulatory jurisdiction.

schema_version.description: [OPTIONAL] Version of the RECORD schema this document conforms to.
